key_insight: Foundational MLIPs can act as a high-accuracy bridge from DFT formation energies to experiment, and their latent features enable even better delta-learning corrections.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical, open-source route to more reliable stability screening of candidate materials without extra costly DFT calculations.
method: Benchmark MC3D formation energies against OQMD, Materials Project, and experimental enthalpies.
Use PET-OMATPES, a foundational MLIP trained at the r2SCAN level, to correct formation energies.
Train delta-learning models on MLIP latent features to further reduce error and preserve phase-stability trends.
evidence_signal: Across the benchmarked crystal datasets, the MLIP correction reduced mean absolute error by over 40% relative to GGA, and latent-feature delta-learning pushed it below 50 meV/atom.
watch_item: The correction quality may depend on the coverage of the MLIP’s training domain and careful regularization to avoid distorting relative phase stability.
paper_title: Correcting DFT formation energies towards experimental accuracy using foundational MLIPs and latent-feature delta-learning
